determinism_check.caveat: GPU determinism is harder than CPU due to CUDA allocator caching across cells. All must-match counters (oom_count, rebalance_count, bytes_migrated_total, current_kv/ssm_pool_bytes, auto_rebalance_skipped_throttle, bytes_wasted_to_alignment_total, current_pressure_state_code, kv/ssm_free_ratio) are byte-identical between primary and rerun. peak_reserved_bytes and fragmentation_during_load drift up to 77.8% across cells because torch.cuda.memory_reserved is dominated by the caching allocator state inherited from earlier cells in the same sweep. The allocator logic is deterministic; the float-metric drift is a harness property.
determinism_check.method: Independent rerun (avmp_dynamic_mr05 variant, capped at 4 GiB to match primary), compared to primary aggregated_deterministic.json rows filtered to variant_label == avmp_dynamic_mr05.
determinism_check.result_counters: byte-identical (all 11 must-match counter fields match across all 12 rows)
determinism_check.result_floats: peak_reserved_bytes_mean drifts max 77.8% / mean 16.7%; fragmentation_during_load_mean drifts max 77.8% / mean 11.9%. Both exceed the 2% threshold from the brief. Per the brief: counters-match-with-float-drift is acceptable, documented and proceeding.

downgrade_reason: RTX 3060 has 12 GiB VRAM; ~1.8 GiB is used by Xwayland and other processes. AVMP allocates 2x total_bytes (RFC 0002 section 4.3). 4 GiB pool gives 8 GiB physical with comfortable headroom; 6 GiB would peak at 12 GiB and exceed the budget. The default (1, 4, 8) GiB total_bytes_options were clamped via --max-total-bytes to (1, 4) GiB after dedup.

smoke_check.comment: avmp_dynamic isolated 4 GiB peak is exactly 2x pool (no caching overhead). The smaller 1 GiB smoke peak of 2970 included ~970 MiB caching residue from prior cells.

success_criterion.c1_passed: uniform_short and agentic_burst (same workloads as PR #14 CPU)
success_criterion.c3_note: avmp_dynamic_mr05 LOSES on peak_reserved against baselines on every workload (9216 MiB vs 5120 MiB on 4 GiB cells). This is the visible cost of the 2x physical footprint design (RFC 0002 section 4.3). Expected, not a regression.
success_criterion.criterion: AVMP v2 must achieve total_oom <= min(fixed_dual_mr05.total_oom, fixed_dual_mr09.total_oom) on every workload in the default sweep, with the v2 variant configured_total_bytes not exceeding any baseline configured_total_bytes.
success_criterion.note_c2_incomparable: GPU cross-workload total (552.0) is lower than PR #14 CPU baseline (786.3) but the GPU sweep uses different total_bytes_options ((1, 4) GiB capped, vs (1, 4, 8) GiB on CPU), so C2 is not directly comparable. The pool-size effect dominates the algorithm effect at the cross-workload level.
success_criterion.verdict: CONTINUE. C1 confirmed on GPU (same 2 workloads as PR #14 CPU). C3 cannot satisfy for AVMP given the 2x footprint design. GPU result CONFIRMS the PR #14 CPU verdict.
